content.full_text:
CITY OF WHITTIER 

13230 PENN STREET, WHITTIER, CALIFORNIA 90602-1772 (213) 945-8200

TO BE FAXED 916-327-2026

JENNIFER
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
SACRAMENTO, CALIF

November 14, 1990

Dear Jennifer:

Since my last fax concerning the trolley cars, another "conflict of interest" problem has arisen. Can you review the facts and advise verbally or via fax?

Staff is working on a proposal for a transitional shelter for the homeless, proposed at the southern end of the Church of Our Lady of Guadalupe. The church is the owner of the property and the applicant is the Saint Vincent de Paul Society.

The applications involved are a conditional use permit, zone change, general plan amendment, and a negative declaration of environmental impact. The process includes a public hearing before the Planning Commission and City Council.

The potential conflict involves four of five members of the Planning Commission. Three member are active members of Our Lady of Guadalupe Congregation. One member of the commission is also a member of the Community Resource Center Council which is also directly involved in this proposal.

We are wondering whether, due to the many interest and organizations which each member of the Planning Commissioners are involved in or attend, the Commission could consider the subject project or whether the items should go directly to the council due to the potential of conflict of interest?

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

Richard Jones
City Attorney

Bob Allen
Law Clerk to Mr. Jones
